description: The chart encodes each month's order count through both bar height and bar width simultaneously, causing the perceived visual area (width × height) to grow roughly as the square of the actual value. This makes months with high order counts like September appear dramatically more prominent than their true proportional difference warrants — for instance, September's bar looks many times larger than January's, even though it only has about 4× as many orders. Readers naturally perceive area rather than height alone, leading them to massively overestimate the differences between busy and slow months.
